grant_sections.instructions: Provide a concise summary of your project for a general audience, avoiding technical jargon. Clearly explain the problem, the proposed solution, and its potential impact.
grant_sections.name: general_audience_abstract
grant_sections.title: General Audience Abstract
search_queries: public health implications of melanoma treatment
impact of melanoma research on treatment

grant_sections.instructions: Provide a technical summary of your project, including specific goals, methodologies, and expected outcomes. Use precise language and terminology appropriate for experts in the field.
grant_sections.name: technical_abstract
grant_sections.title: Technical Abstract

grant_sections.instructions: List relevant keywords that describe your project's focus, methodologies, and key areas of research. These keywords should be specific and representative of the project's core elements.
grant_sections.name: project_keywords
grant_sections.title: Keywords

grant_sections.instructions: Provide a detailed background on the problem your research addresses. Clearly state your research question or hypothesis, and specific aims. Use this section to justify your research direction based on the existing scientific literature.
grant_sections.name: background_and_specific_aims
grant_sections.title: Background and Specific Aims

grant_sections.instructions: Describe the potential clinical impact of your research, including how your findings could lead to new treatments, diagnostic methods, or prevention strategies for melanoma. Explain the potential benefits for patients and the broader healthcare system.
grant_sections.name: clinical_impact
grant_sections.title: Potential for Clinical Impact

grant_sections.instructions: Provide a detailed timeline for your project, including major milestones, deadlines for key activities, and expected completion dates. Ensure that the timeline is realistic and achievable within the grant period. If applicable, include patient accrual timelines and timelines for analysis.
grant_sections.name: timeline_and_milestones
grant_sections.title: Timeline and Milestones

grant_sections.instructions: List and describe all key personnel involved in the project, their roles, and their relevant qualifications and experience. For Team Science Awards, specify the Administrative PI, other Principal Investigators, Young Investigator, and Mentor roles, as applicable. Include ORCID IDs for all PIs, Young Investigators, and Mentors. Make sure to detail the unique value of each person's experience and how this will impact the success of the project. This section should highlight the team’s collective expertise and their ability to successfully execute the proposed research.
grant_sections.name: key_personnel
grant_sections.title: Key Personnel
search_queries: roles and responsibilities of principal investigators in research
team science project management best practices
qualifications and experience for research team members
ORCID ID usage in research grant applications
research team expertise and its impact on project success

grant_sections.instructions: List all current and pending support for the Applicant and other relevant Key Personnel (Principal Investigators and Young Investigators for Team Science applications). Describe any overlap with the MRA proposal and explain how any potential overlap will be managed. For Young Investigator and Pilot applications, only list support for the Applicant.
grant_sections.name: current_and_pending_support
grant_sections.title: Current and Pending Support
search_queries: managing financial overlap in research grants

grant_sections.instructions: Describe your plan for data management and sharing, including how you will make your research outputs (publications, data, code, and software) publicly available. Explain your compliance with MRA's data sharing policy, including the use of CC BY-NC licenses and deposition in PubMed Central and other relevant databases.
grant_sections.name: data_sharing_plan
grant_sections.title: Data Sharing Plan
search_queries: open science data sharing policies medical research
research data accessibility and curation methods
CC BY-NC license usage in research publications

grant_sections.instructions: Provide a statement confirming the applicant has an independent research program and will be supported by the institution. Include a brief statement about the applicant, the Mentor's role, mentoring plan, the research environment, and sources of institutional support that the applicant will utilize in conducting the project.
grant_sections.name: institutional_commitment
grant_sections.title: Institutional Commitment

mentoring plan for early career researchers in STEM
research environment resources for scientific projects
availability of resources for research projects
institutional commitment to research training and development
scientific infrastructure support for grant recipients
resource allocation strategies for scientific projects

grant_sections.instructions: Provide a concise statement (up to 2000 characters) of how the proposed research project fits within MRA's overall research portfolio. Explain how the proposed research addresses MRA's strategic priorities and will contribute to melanoma research advancement.
grant_sections.name: fit_with_mra_portfolio
grant_sections.title: Fit with MRA Research Portfolio
